vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but fails to account for the severe decline of the specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor, but the foliage is in a state of advanced necrosis. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot but shows signs of extreme dehydration. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are largely indistinguishable or absent, suggesting a systemic loss of these specific specimens.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss - No viable mint foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Black Pot) - High necrosis, near-total loss of healthy tissue.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss - No viable silver guest foliage detected.

plant_audit.P1: Stems are thin and desiccated; leaf turgor is non-existent.
plant_audit.P2: No identifiable mint growth; soil surface appears barren.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos leaf is chlorotic and necrotic; the white rabbit anchor remains, but the plant is failing.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white perlite/debris; no fungal growth observed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Minimal change; initial signs of leaf yellowing on P3.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-3: Progressive browning of P3 leaf; P1 shows signs of thinning.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_current: Stagnation of growth; P3 leaf has reached a state of near-total necrosis. No recovery observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of turgor in all specimens indicates a chronic water deficit or root system collapse. The Pothos (P3) is effectively terminal.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, dying leaf in P3 suggests a failed propagation or a dying remnant of a larger plant. No new growth or interventions detected.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a collection in severe distress. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from advanced chlorosis and necrosis, likely due to improper moisture regulation. The String of Nickels (P1) is severely dehydrated. The absence of P2 and P4 suggests these specimens have perished and been removed or decomposed entirely. The environment is stable but the biological occupants are not thriving.
